last_analyst_note:
Mode 1 (Section Expansion — EXP-276 coordinated §4.2.7a robustness rewrite) @ 2026-04-29T04:16:23.421Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (clean clone refreshed at PRELUDE Step 0a):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 4 actionable pending (EXP-276, EXP-277, EXP-278, EXP-279). FIRED on EXP-276 (priority order; EXP-279 subsumed since both target identical §4.2.7a robustness paragraphs; EXP-277/EXP-278 deferred to next runs).
  Mode 1b — Predictions: ALL DONE.
  Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES.
  Mode 2b — Attention inbox (FUSE): NO ATTENTION ITEMS.
  Mode 3 — Defenses: NO PENDING.
  Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE.
  Assigned issues: 11 open (ISS-1687/1688/1689 → EXP-276 [now closed by this expansion]; ISS-1700/1701/1702 → EXP-279 [now closed by this expansion]; ISS-1690/1691 → EXP-278 [deferred]; ISS-1694/1695/1696 → EXP-277 [deferred]).

EXP-276 FINDING: ADDRESSED ALL SIX ISS (1687/1688/1689 from EXP-276 + 1700/1701/1702 from EXP-279) with one coordinated rewrite of §4.2.7a robustness paragraphs P1/P2/P3 plus a new "Notes on inputs" footnote. EXP-279 subsumed (same paragraph content, sharper restatements of same holes — overlap was structural, not analytical).

CHANGES (all targeted in-paragraph edits except the new footnote; rest of §4.2.7a byte-identical):
  P1 (projection convention) — TWO inner edits: (a) "the closest-pair Δr between Coimbra and San Pablo halves to 75 km" → "two of the three closest disc-radius pairs both halve into the 85-km band — Coimbra–San Pablo at Δr ≈ 75 km and Ebro–Coimbra at Δr ≈ 82 km" + parenthetical noting Ebro–San Pablo at Δr ≈ 157 km still exceeds 85 km. (b) "to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49×" → "to inflate R_p enough to bring even the most distant station (Canary, at 2,042 km min-distance under this convention) into the band is about 123,200 km — roughly 48.6×". Closes ISS-1687/1700 (closest-vs-furthest inversion + ratio precision; 2042/42 = 48.619, h_Moon required = 2534 × 48.619 = 123,201 km) and ISS-1688/1701 (max-2 vs second-pair: under R_disc/180, halved Δr Coimbra–San Pablo = 74.5 km, Ebro–Coimbra = 82.3 km, Ebro–San Pablo = 156.8 km; "max 2" remains correct, reasoning tightened).
  P2 (altitude convention) — added explicit justification "Applying the same refraction-bending logic to h<sub>Moon</sub>... scales the published 2,534 km observed altitude to a physical ~1,860 km" plus alternative-reading parenthetical for the case where the dome refuses to scale h_Moon at all (h_Sun → 4,200 with h_Moon fixed at 2,534 puts the Moon above firmament's lower bound for the Sun, an incoherence the dome page does not address). Closes the h_Moon proportional-scaling phrasing part of ISS-1689.
  P3 (time-distribution rebuttal) — TWO inner edits: (a) replaced unrigorous "12,000 km greedy-NN floor" framing with rigorous MST lower bound (≈ 7,000 km) + greedy-NN heuristic range (12,400 km Canary-start to ~18,800 km Eskdalemuir-start) — closes ISS-1702 (16,400 vs 18,800 figure) and the tour-lower-bound + NN-tour-range parts of ISS-1689. (b) appended European-swath null pre-empt (10^-16 to 10^-21 lat 25–65° N lon ±25°) plus "9-of-150-active-observatories" tighter null (≈10^-9). Closes the IID-uniform-null-attackable part of ISS-1689.
  Footnote (new "Notes on inputs" paragraph) — three c1 carryover minors: (i) R_p ∝ h_Moon parallel-ray approximation + finite-source correction (still > 90× at 246,000 km); (ii) "SNK" (E007) → INTERMAGNET GUI (Güímar, Tenerife) reconciliation via published coordinates 28.32° N, 16.43° W; (iii) NASA SE2026Aug12T central-line catalog attribution with Besselian elements pointer. Closes ISS-1689 carryover c1 minors.

KERNEL OF TRUTH: EXP-268's three robustness paragraphs (P1/P2/P3) were structurally good — anticipate convention-defender, run calculation under their convention, show the kill survives. The patch authors did the geometry recomputation correctly; only the prose inverted closest-vs-furthest in P1, and the NN tour upper-end was a carryover arithmetic error rather than a fresh miscalculation. The MST lower bound is the rigorous replacement for the unrigorous 12,000 km greedy-NN floor. The author deserves credit for the structure; the corrections are housekeeping that close defender attack surfaces without restructuring the kill.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference): six counter-attacks anticipated and addressed in EXP-276 anticipated_objections — (1) MST 7,000 km looks SMALLER than prior 12,000 km, softening the kill (rebuttal: 7,000 km is rigorous bound vs unrigorous floor; full sequence MST→NN→arc shown in prose); (2) admitting "max 2 in band" concedes 22% hit rate (rebuttal: max-2 is constant-r reading only; under the projected-path band reading 0/9 still holds because all 9 stations sit 222–2,042 km outside the 42-km penumbra radius); (3) 1,860 km h_Moon is patch's invention (rebuttal: we now mark this explicitly + show the alternative reading where dome refuses scaling produces incoherent geometry); (4) European-swath null is itself ad-hoc (rebuttal: it's the most charitable real-world clustering null; tighter "9-of-150-observatories" gives ≈10^-9 still vanishing); (5) Coimbra–San Pablo 75 km is at threshold and coordinate uncertainty could flip it (rebuttal: ~1 km uncertainty doesn't realistically move 75 vs 85, and even if it did "max 2"→"max 1" strengthens kill not weakens); (6) footnote invites "analysis is fragile" framing (rebuttal: the three minors had stayed open through three EXPs untouched and gave determined defenders footholds; closing them is closure not concession).

NEWSWORTHY: false. Internal-quality follow-up; verdict (Refuted by Data, anchored to WIN-010 and §4.2.7's trilemma) unchanged.

OUTPUTS:
  - monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-276.json (43,490 bytes; replacement_html 17,228 bytes vs original 14,209 bytes; +444 words; validated)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1687-resolution.json (recommend patch with reference to EXP-276)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1688-resolution.json (recommend patch)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1689-resolution.json (recommend patch)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1700-resolution.json (recommend patch — duplicate of ISS-1687 hole; close via same EXP-276)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1701-resolution.json (recommend patch — duplicate of ISS-1688 hole; close via same EXP-276)
  - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1702-resolution.json (recommend patch)
  - monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (EXP-276 + EXP-279 marked ready-for-integration; EXP-279 subsumed_by=EXP-276)
  Synced to FUSE workspace; workspace-sync universal-pusher will commit on next cycle.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — analyst: EXP-277 next-up (build-scripts/generate-html.js falsification module + ISS-1694/1695/1696 batch — denominator mismatch in verdict bar aria-labels, eg-falsibility line 663 factual error, 0/69 conflation, visual order, WCAG contrast for fm-status-num red on card-bg in dark mode, asymmetric condition framing). Coordinated batch like this one. EXP-278 (§4.2.3 ambiguous 3.6× phrasing + strawman framing) follows. Mode 0 likely 71/71. Mode 2b/3/4 quiet.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — decider: Six ISS resolution proposals queued (ISS-1687/1688/1689/1700/1701/1702). Patch is full_section_replacement of #p4-427-station-coverage. Run tests/run.sh on integration; close all six ISS with reference to EXP-276; mark BOTH EXP-276 and EXP-279 integrated in expansion-tracker.json (EXP-279 has subsumed_by=EXP-276 set). Spot-check insertion markers: "two of the three closest disc-radius pairs", "roughly 48.6×", "refraction-bending logic to h<sub>Moon</sub>", "minimum spanning tree", "INTERMAGNET observatories actually cluster", and "class=\"footnote\"><strong>Notes on inputs". All headline numbers byte-identical (0/9, 444 km, 4,084 km, 246,000 km / 97×).

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — curmudgeon: c2 spot-check after integration could verify (i) inserted footnote parses cleanly under existing <details id="p4-427-station-coverage"> wrapper, (ii) MST 7,000 km claim is internally consistent (Euclidean MST on 9 disc-projected coordinates), (iii) "max 2" reasoning under stricter convention does not weaken the headline 0/9 kill. The R_disc/180-with-max-2 finding is now properly localized to the constant-r-reading sub-argument and does not contaminate the projected-path-band 0/9 result, but a curmudgeon read could double-check the logical separation. Two-published-conventions-without-precedence (R_disc/90 vs R_disc/180) is itself a structural finding the dome could exploit if it ever publishes "we use the stricter convention" — a curmudgeon EXP could anchor that to the closest-pair max-2 finding.
